affordances.name: Reveal the right whole before detail
affordances.mechanism: When comprehension depends on how people group, contrast, or orient to information, use Gestalt principles to make the right structure visible while treating the pattern as provisional, not causal proof.

use_when: People need structure fast before they can use detail.
Design, explanation, or presentation quality depends on whether the right elements are perceived as together or apart.
A complex idea needs a coherent organizing shape to reduce cognitive load.
do_not_use_when: A satisfying pattern would be mistaken for complete truth or causal proof.
The case requires precision in details before high-level grouping.
The model would reinforce an initial frame instead of testing it.
case_evidence_needed: The elements people currently misgroup, miss, or confuse.
The desired whole, contrast, hierarchy, or grouping.
The task or decision the perceptual structure must support.
The evidence that the grouping reflects real relationships rather than aesthetic satisfaction.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to use visual or conceptual grouping to orient attention while adding a caveat that the pattern must be checked against evidence.
evidence_required: The grouping or contrast that should become salient.
The task or decision improved by the grouping.
A check against false pattern recognition.
good_output_shape: Explains why the grouping helps the task.
Keeps the design or explanation concrete.
Warns that pattern clarity is not proof.

diagnostic_questions: What should be perceived as one whole, and what should stand apart?
What decision or task does this grouping help?
What evidence would show the pleasing pattern is misleading?
misuse_guards: Do not treat perceptual grouping as causal evidence.
Do not let visual or conceptual neatness hide crucial detail.
Do not lock onto the first recognized pattern without testing alternatives.

source_evidence.source_quote: perception occurs in wholes before it registers component parts
Best when design should reveal the right grouping
satisfying perceptual organization is treated as causal proof

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 15 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports Gestalt as orientation and grouping discipline with anti-pattern checks.
dropped_material: Did not extract decorative visual-design advice.
Did not treat Gestalt grouping as evidence of causal structure.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card improves presentation and explanation packets without becoming aesthetic vocabulary.

absence_records.reason: The source supports Gestalt as a cognitive and decision-orientation model, not a generic aesthetic preference.
The source anchors grouping to comprehension, design, communication, and decision tasks; it does not support grouping divorced from the work it enables.

source_evidence.source_quote: quality of the outcome depends on whether the brain naturally perceives the important elements

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 15 source reading found strong reviewed depth for perceptual structure with false-pattern guardrails.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: A later packet should check whether the card helps distinguish orientation from proof.
